phases.name: Slice 1+2 — Scheduler placement function and ProcessDriver (parallelisable; independent crates with no dep edge)

steps.name: Slice 1 — first-fit scheduler: pure deterministic schedule(...) in overdrive-scheduler crate
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from US-01 (discuss/user-stories.md):
- pub fn schedule(nodes: &BTreeMap<NodeId, Node>, job: &Job, current_allocs: &[AllocStatusRow]) -> Result<NodeId, PlacementError> exists in overdrive-scheduler crate (NEW, class core per ADR-0024) as a pure synchronous function with NO `.await`, NO `Instant::now`, NO `SystemTime::now`, NO `rand::*`, NO `tokio::time::sleep`
- PlacementError enum carries NoCapacity { needed: Resources, max_free: Resources } and NoHealthyNode (taint variants explicitly OUT — Phase 1 single-node)
- All internal iteration is via BTreeMap per `.claude/rules/development.md` ordered-collection rule (dst-lint enforces — overdrive-scheduler is class core)
- free_capacity(node, allocs) -> Resources helper handles zero-capacity edge without numeric underflow (saturating_sub or equivalent)
- proptest covers determinism: 1024 cases, identical-input → identical-output Result<NodeId, PlacementError>
- proptest covers BTreeMap-order invariance: same set under different insertion orders produces equal Result
- xtask dst-lint scan reports zero violations against overdrive-scheduler
- Doc-comment on `schedule` lists the determinism contract and BTreeMap-only iteration rule

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md, all @US-01):
- 1.1 first-fit happy path → tests/acceptance/first_fit_happy_path.rs::scheduler_picks_local_node_when_capacity_fits
- 1.2 determinism proptest → tests/acceptance/determinism.rs::scheduler_is_deterministic_under_proptest
- 1.3 BTreeMap-order invariance proptest → tests/acceptance/determinism.rs::scheduler_is_invariant_under_btreemap_insertion_order
- 1.4 NoCapacity subtracts running allocs → tests/acceptance/capacity_accounting.rs::scheduler_subtracts_running_allocs_from_capacity
- 1.5 NoCapacity reports needed+max_free on memory exhaustion → tests/acceptance/capacity_accounting.rs::scheduler_reports_needed_and_max_free_on_memory_exhaustion
- 1.6 empty node set → NoHealthyNode → tests/acceptance/empty_node_set.rs::scheduler_returns_no_healthy_node_for_empty_input
- 1.7 zero-capacity edge no underflow → tests/acceptance/capacity_accounting.rs::scheduler_handles_zero_capacity_without_underflow
- 1.8 dst-lint clean (xtask test) → xtask/tests/dst_lint_scope.rs::overdrive_scheduler_passes_dst_lint

KPI contribution: K1 (scheduler determinism, 100% identical-input identical-output across 1024 proptest cases; zero HashMap occurrences in hot path).

Disproves (from slice-1 brief):
- "The scheduler needs DB-backed memory or async I/O." → No, first-fit is pure over (nodes_view, job, current_allocs).
- "BTreeMap is heavy enough to want HashMap." → No, Phase 1 has N=1 node; the determinism gain dwarfs iteration cost.
- "With one node, we don't need a real scheduler." → No, the predicate must exist as a known-shape pure function under DST replay so Phase 2+ multi-node is a content change, not structural.
steps.description: Slice 1 of the carpaccio plan. Replaces the panic!('Not yet implemented -- RED scaffold') body of `overdrive_scheduler::schedule` with the real first-fit placement function. Lands the PlacementError enum and the free_capacity helper. After GREEN, scheduler RED scaffolds are gone; ProcessDriver (entry 01-02) and the lifecycle reconciler (entry 02-01) remain RED. Mechanically independent of 01-02 — different crates, no dep edge — so the two phase-01 entries are parallelisable per the slice gating in design/wave-decisions.md.

steps.name: Slice 2 — ProcessDriver: tokio::process spawn + cgroups v2 scope (overdrive-worker, integration-tests-gated, Linux-only)
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from US-02 (discuss/user-stories.md):
- ProcessDriver struct in crates/overdrive-worker/src/driver/process.rs (per ADR-0029 — relocated from overdrive-host by the 2026-04-27 worker-crate extraction amendment) implements the Driver trait
- Driver::start spawns via tokio::process::Command and creates `overdrive.slice/workloads.slice/<alloc_id>.scope` via direct cgroupfs writes (per ADR-0026 D9; no cgroups-rs dep)
- AllocationHandle carries PID and CgroupPath (NEW STRICT newtype in overdrive-worker)
- CgroupPath: FromStr (validating, rejects path-traversal `../`, `//`, leading `/`), Display, serde + rkyv derives, full proptest round-trip
- Driver::status polls process liveness and returns AllocationState (Running, Terminated, etc.)
- Driver::stop sends SIGTERM, waits configurable grace, escalates to SIGKILL if needed; removes cgroup scope after process is reaped
- cpu.weight (in [1, 10000] derived from cpu_milli) and memory.max are written from AllocationSpec.resources BEFORE the PID is placed in cgroup.procs (ADR-0026 D9)
- Limit-write failure produces a structured warning log but does NOT fail the start (warn-and-continue per ADR-0026 D9)
- Linux-only integration test under `integration-tests` feature actually starts /bin/sleep, asserts /proc/<pid>/cgroup shows the expected scope, then stops cleanly
- Default-lane (no integration-tests feature) tests do NOT spawn real processes — SimDriver is the fixture
- Missing-binary path: returns DriverError::SpawnFailed (or StartRejected) WITHOUT creating an orphaned cgroup scope
- dst-lint does not flag overdrive-worker (class adapter-host per ADR-0029, exempt from scan)

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md, all @US-02):
- 2.1 default lane uses SimDriver only → tests/acceptance/sim_driver_only_in_default_lane.rs::default_lane_does_not_spawn_real_processes
- 2.2 (WALKING SKELETON extension) ProcessDriver starts /bin/sleep + cgroup scope → tests/integration/process_driver/start_and_running.rs::process_driver_starts_real_sleep_in_cgroup_scope
- 2.3 child PID lands in cgroup.procs → tests/integration/process_driver/cgroup_procs.rs::child_pid_appears_in_cgroup_procs
- 2.4 cpu.weight + memory.max written from spec → tests/integration/process_driver/resource_enforcement.rs::cpu_weight_and_memory_max_are_written_from_spec
- 2.5 missing binary → no orphaned cgroup → tests/integration/process_driver/missing_binary.rs::missing_binary_does_not_create_cgroup_scope
- 2.6 stop with grace → Terminated + scope removed → tests/integration/process_driver/stop_with_grace.rs::stop_with_grace_drives_to_terminated_and_removes_scope
- 2.7 SIGTERM ignored → SIGKILL escalation → tests/integration/process_driver/stop_escalates_to_sigkill.rs::stop_escalates_to_sigkill_when_sigterm_ignored
- 2.8 limit-write failure warns and continues → tests/integration/process_driver/limit_write_failure_warns.rs::limit_write_failure_warns_and_continues
- 2.9 CgroupPath round-trips proptest → tests/acceptance/cgroup_path_roundtrip.rs::cgroup_path_roundtrips_for_every_valid_input
- 2.10 CgroupPath rejects traversal characters → tests/acceptance/cgroup_path_validation.rs::cgroup_path_rejects_traversal_characters

KPI contribution: K2 (100% of Driver::start integration runs produce process whose /proc/<pid>/cgroup matches the AllocationHandle's cgroup path; 0 zombie processes after Driver::stop; STRICT CgroupPath newtype with full FromStr/Display/serde/rkyv/proptest coverage).

Disproves (from slice-2 brief):
- "We need a separate cgroup-management abstraction layer above Driver." → No, the driver IS the cgroup-aware spawn site.
- "Process spawn can be hidden behind Driver without leaking into core's compile path." → Test under integration-tests gate; default lane stays pure-Rust against SimDriver.

Linux-only constraint: macOS dev hosts run default lane against SimDriver and must run integration tests in a Linux VM (matches Tier 3 testing pattern in `.claude/rules/testing.md`). Windows is explicitly out of scope for Phase 1.
steps.description: Slice 2 of the carpaccio plan. Replaces the panic!('Not yet implemented -- RED scaffold') bodies in overdrive-worker's ProcessDriver impl. Walking-skeleton extension scenario 2.2 is the highest-level acceptance test. Lands the CgroupPath STRICT newtype (the new domain identifier introduced by this feature). After GREEN, ProcessDriver RED scaffolds are gone; the action shim and lifecycle reconciler (entry 02-01) remain RED. Mechanically independent of 01-01 — different crates, no dep edge.
steps.time_estimate: ~1 day (Linux developer; ~2 days if developer is on macOS and needs Linux VM in the loop)

phases.name: Slice 3 — Lifecycle reconciler closes the convergence loop (depends on 01-01 and 01-02)

steps.name: Slice 3 — JobLifecycle reconciler + action shim + AppState driver wiring + 3 DST invariants + `overdrive job stop` end-to-end
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from US-03 (discuss/user-stories.md):
- Action::{StartAllocation, StopAllocation, RestartAllocation} variants exist on the Action enum (additive on the existing enum in overdrive-core)
- JobLifecycle reconciler struct + JobLifecycleView (libSQL-hydrated; restart_counts: BTreeMap<AllocationId, u32>, next_attempt_at: BTreeMap<AllocationId, Instant>)
- AnyReconciler::JobLifecycle(JobLifecycle) + AnyReconcilerView::JobLifecycle(JobLifecycleView) variants exist with match arms in name, hydrate, reconcile (typed AnyState pattern per ADR-0021)
- Action shim (NEW runtime layer in overdrive-control-plane per ADR-0023): consumes Vec<Action> from runtime.drain(), dispatches allocation-management actions to Arc<dyn Driver>, writes AllocStatusRow to ObservationStore. Lives in overdrive-control-plane::reconciler_runtime::action_shim.
- AppState extended with driver: Arc<dyn Driver> field (ADR-0022)
- Lifecycle reconciler is registered at boot via runtime.register(job_lifecycle())? in run_server_with_obs_and_driver (ADR-0025)
- DST invariant `JobScheduledAfterSubmission` exists in overdrive-sim::invariants::evaluators and passes (eventually invariant: a submitted job becomes Running within N ticks)
- DST invariant `DesiredReplicaCountConverges` exists and passes (eventually invariant: count(state == Running) == job.replicas for each submitted job)
- DST invariant `NoDoubleScheduling` exists and passes (always invariant: each allocation appears under exactly one node_id; vacuous-pass shape under N=1; the invariant still has to hold)
- Existing `ReconcilerIsPure` invariant continues to pass with JobLifecycle in the catalogue (twin invocation produces equal (Vec<Action>, NextView))
- xtask dst-lint reports no banned API in JobLifecycle::reconcile body: NO Instant::now, NO SystemTime::now, NO rand::*, NO tokio::time::sleep, NO `.await` inside reconcile. tick.now is the only wall-clock source.
- `overdrive job stop <id>` CLI subcommand exists in overdrive-cli
- HTTP handler `POST /v1/jobs/{id}:stop` exists per ADR-0027 (writes IntentKey::for_job_stop record, preserving the original spec under IntentKey::for_job byte-for-byte)
- Lifecycle reconciler reads stopped intent and emits Action::StopAllocation for each running allocation; action shim dispatches to Driver::stop; allocation transitions Running → Draining → Terminated
- `overdrive job stop` is idempotent (already-stopped job returns code 0 + 'already stopped' output)
- `overdrive job stop unknown` returns 404 + non-zero exit + actionable stderr
- `overdrive alloc status` Pending-row rendering surfaces PlacementError::NoCapacity reason text actionably (start-side counterpart to stop-side affordance)
- `overdrive cluster status` lists both `noop-heartbeat` and `job-lifecycle` reconcilers
- Backoff exhaustion: after M restart attempts (configured ceiling) the reconciler emits no further StartAllocation; allocation enters Failed (backoff exhausted)

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md, all @US-03):
- 3.1 (WALKING SKELETON) submitted 1-replica job converges to Running via real ProcessDriver → tests/integration/job_lifecycle/submit_to_running.rs::submitted_job_reaches_running_via_real_process_driver
- 3.2 reconciler is pure (DST property) → crates/overdrive-sim/tests/acceptance/reconciler_is_pure_with_job_lifecycle.rs::job_lifecycle_satisfies_reconciler_is_pure_invariant
- 3.3 dst-lint clean on reconcile body → xtask/tests/dst_lint_scope.rs::job_lifecycle_reconcile_body_passes_dst_lint
- 3.4 DST invariant JobScheduledAfterSubmission → crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators/job_scheduled_after_submission.rs::evaluate (plus harness coverage in cargo xtask dst)
- 3.5 DST invariant DesiredReplicaCountConverges → crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators/desired_replica_count_converges.rs::evaluate
- 3.6 DST invariant NoDoubleScheduling → crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators/no_double_scheduling.rs::evaluate
- 3.7 (WALKING SKELETON) killed workload restarted with fresh alloc_id → tests/integration/job_lifecycle/crash_recovery.rs::killed_workload_is_restarted_with_fresh_alloc_id
- 3.8 backoff exhaustion (default lane via SimDriver) → tests/acceptance/job_lifecycle_backoff.rs::repeatedly_crashing_workload_exhausts_backoff_and_stops_retrying
- 3.9 (WALKING SKELETON) `overdrive job stop` drives Running → Terminated → tests/integration/job_lifecycle/stop_to_terminated.rs::job_stop_drives_running_to_terminated
- 3.10 stop is idempotent → tests/acceptance/job_stop_idempotent.rs::stop_on_already_stopped_job_returns_already_stopped_outcome
- 3.11 stop on unknown job returns 404 → tests/acceptance/job_stop_unknown.rs::stop_on_unknown_job_returns_404
- 3.12 :stop writes separate IntentKey preserving spec → tests/acceptance/job_stop_intent_key.rs::stop_writes_separate_intent_key_preserving_spec
- 3.13 Pending row surfaces NoCapacity reason actionably → tests/acceptance/pending_no_capacity_renders_reason.rs::pending_renders_no_capacity_reason_actionably
- 3.14 (WALKING SKELETON) cluster status lists both reconcilers → tests/acceptance/cluster_status_lists_both_reconcilers.rs::cluster_status_renders_job_lifecycle_alongside_noop_heartbeat

KPI contribution: K3 (100% of submitted 1-replica jobs reach Running within N ticks under DST; 100% of `job stop` calls drive allocation to Terminated within N+M ticks; 0 reconciler purity regressions per PR; 100% crash-recovery scenarios converge back to Running within N+M ticks bounded by libSQL backoff state). Three new DST invariants gated in CI; existing ReconcilerIsPure covers the new reconciler; end-to-end integration test under integration-tests submits real job, asserts Running, kills process, asserts recovery, then `job stop` asserts Terminated.

Disproves (from slice-3 brief):
- "The lifecycle reconciler must perform I/O — it can't really be pure." → No, the action shim is the I/O boundary; reconciler stays pure (matches Anvil reconcile_core + shim per OSDI '24).
- "We need to invent a new abstraction to dispatch Actions." → No, action shim is just a runtime function: take Action, switch on variant, call Driver if allocation-management, write AllocStatusRow.
- "State can stay opaque forever." → No, this slice forces the structural blocker open; ADR-0021 (typed AnyState pattern) ratifies the resolution.
- "`job stop` belongs in a separate slice from convergence." → No, stop is the inverse of start; both pass through the same lifecycle reconciler + action shim path.

Reconciler purity (from System Constraints in user-stories.md):
- Lifecycle reconciler MUST satisfy existing ReconcilerIsPure DST invariant
- NO `.await`, NO wall-clock reads, NO direct store writes inside reconcile
- Wall-clock comes from tick.now (snapshot at evaluation start)
- Action shim is the I/O boundary; reconciler is not
- All internal collections in reconcile MUST be BTreeMap (overdrive-control-plane is class adapter-host so dst-lint does NOT scan, but the discipline still applies — the reconciler should be portable to a core-class crate)
steps.description: Slice 3 of the carpaccio plan — the convergence loop closes. Replaces the panic!('Not yet implemented -- RED scaffold') bodies for JobLifecycle reconciler, AnyReconciler::JobLifecycle match arms, action_shim dispatch, AppState::driver wiring, the three DST invariant evaluators, and the `overdrive job stop` CLI + handler. Walking-skeleton extension scenario 3.1 is the highest-level acceptance test. After GREEN, the only RED scaffold remaining is the control-plane cgroup pre-flight (entry 03-01). HIGHEST RISK ENTRY: bundles scheduler call + action shim + 3 DST invariants + AppState migration + `overdrive job stop` end-to-end. Slice taste-test rates ≤4 components as BORDERLINE (6 conceptual components: reconciler, AnyReconciler/View extension, action shim, AppState extension, 3 Action variants, 3 DST invariants, job stop CLI+handler) — acceptable because each is small and the shim wires them all, with the stop side mirroring the start side. The DESIGN-resolved State shape (ADR-0021 typed AnyState) is ratified before this entry begins; the scaffold is in place. If during execution this entry feels too dense, escalate to the user — the DISCUSS-flagged escape hatch is to split into 3A (start path) + 3B (stop path) without restructuring the JSON.
steps.time_estimate: ~1-2 days (upper end; bundles new variants + new runtime layer + new DST invariants + stop-and-drain end-to-end)

phases.name: Slice 4 — Control-plane cgroup isolation (depends on 01-02 ProcessDriver and 02-01 lifecycle integration test fixture)

steps.name: Slice 4 — control-plane cgroup v2 pre-flight + bootstrap slice creation + responsiveness-under-burst integration test
steps.criteria:
AC inherited from US-04 (discuss/user-stories.md):
- cgroup v2 delegation pre-flight check exists in the `overdrive serve` boot path
- Pre-flight refuses to start (exit non-zero, no /v1 endpoint binds) when:
  - cgroup v2 is unavailable (cgroup v1 host; no cgroup2 in /proc/filesystems)
  - cgroup v2 is mounted but neither cpu nor memory is delegated to running UID
  - cgroup v2 is delegated but cpu (or memory) is missing from cgroup.subtree_control
- Server creates `overdrive.slice/control-plane.slice/` (idempotent — existing slice is fine; reuses on a second boot)
- Server enrols its own PID into the control-plane slice via cgroup.procs at boot
- Pre-flight error messages answer 'what / why / how to fix' per nw-ux-tui-patterns: name the missing controller, name the kernel version on cgroup v1, mention the systemd `Delegate=yes` fix and the `--allow-no-cgroups` dev escape hatch, point to the operations doc URL
- `overdrive serve --allow-no-cgroups` dev escape hatch: bypasses the pre-flight, logs a WARNING banner naming dev-only disposition, binds the listener, subsequent Driver::start calls skip cgroup scope creation
- Linux-only integration test (gated `integration-tests` feature) submits a CPU-burst workload (e.g. stress --cpu N) and asserts `overdrive cluster status` returns within 100 ms during the burst
- Server-boot smoke test reads /proc/self/cgroup at boot and asserts the path includes `overdrive.slice/control-plane.slice/`
- Existing default-lane tests do NOT require cgroup v2 delegation (pre-flight gated behind `integration-tests` feature OR via `--allow-no-cgroups` for in-process server fixtures per ADR-0028)

Scenarios driving RED→GREEN (from distill/test-scenarios.md, all @US-04):
- 4.1 (WALKING SKELETON) server boot enrols PID in slice → tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/server_enrols_in_slice.rs::server_boot_enrols_running_pid_in_control_plane_slice
- 4.2 (WALKING SKELETON) cluster status responsive under workload CPU burst → tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/cluster_status_under_burst.rs::cluster_status_remains_responsive_under_workload_cpu_burst
- 4.3 idempotent slice creation (second boot reuses) → tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/idempotent_slice_creation.rs::server_boot_reuses_existing_control_plane_slice
- 4.4 refuses without delegation → tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/preflight_no_delegation.rs::server_refuses_start_when_subtree_control_lacks_cpu_and_memory
- 4.5 refuses on cgroup v1 → tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/preflight_v1_host.rs::server_refuses_start_on_cgroup_v1_host
- 4.6 names missing cpu controller specifically → tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/preflight_missing_cpu.rs::preflight_names_missing_cpu_controller
- 4.7 --allow-no-cgroups bypass with warning banner → tests/integration/cgroup_isolation/allow_no_cgroups_flag.rs::allow_no_cgroups_flag_bypasses_preflight_with_warning_banner
- 4.8 default lane has no cgroup dependency → tests/acceptance/default_lane_no_cgroup_dependency.rs::default_lane_does_not_depend_on_cgroup_v2_delegation

KPI contribution: K4 (100% integration test runs show `cluster status` returning < 100 ms during 100% CPU workload burst; 0 control-plane-starvation regressions across PRs touching the slice machinery; pre-flight refusal gives actionable error per nw-ux-tui-patterns).

Disproves (from slice-4 brief):
- "Slice creation can wait for systemd unit packaging in DEVOPS." → No, in-process bootstrap is the SSOT for slice topology; deferring leaves control plane unprotected during dev usage.
- "On a single-node co-located host, cgroup isolation is a Phase 2+ luxury." → No, runaway process on the same host as the control plane is exactly the case whitepaper §4 calls out; the integration test IS the disproof attempt — if workload starves control plane in the burst test, the structural claim is paper.

GH #20 split (recorded in user-stories.md and design/wave-decisions.md):
- This entry covers ONLY the control-plane cgroup-isolation half of GH #20
- Scheduler taint/toleration support is explicitly DEFERRED — Phase 1 is single-node, taint/toleration delivers no value with N=1 nodes
- User is expected to split GH #20 into two issues with the latter scheduled for the multi-node phase

Linux-only constraint: macOS / Windows hosts cannot run this slice's integration test; default-lane tests must not depend on cgroup v2 (per AC bullet on default-lane gating).
steps.description: Slice 4 of the carpaccio plan — the structural backstop for control-plane responsiveness under workload pressure. Replaces the panic!('Not yet implemented -- RED scaffold') bodies for the cgroup pre-flight checker, the server-bootstrap CgroupManager, and the --allow-no-cgroups dev flag wiring. The walking-skeleton scenario 4.2 (cluster status under workload CPU burst) is the highest-level acceptance test — it is the integration test that disproves the 'paper guarantee' attack on whitepaper §4. After GREEN, all RED scaffolds for Phase 1 first-workload are gone; the feature is complete. Depends on entry 01-02 (ProcessDriver wiring; this entry extends cgroup symmetry to the control plane) and entry 02-01 (lifecycle reconciler producing the real workload to assert against in the burst integration test).
steps.time_estimate: ~1 day (Linux developer; integration test depends on real kernel)

validation.notes: APPROVED-WITH-NOTES. All 39 scenarios mapped (8/10/14/8). 7 walking-skeleton scenarios placed correctly across phases 01/02/03. Dependency graph parallelises 01-01||01-02 then 02-01 then 03-01. ADR coverage comprehensive. Slice 3 sizing (14 scenarios, 6 components) acknowledged as HIGHEST RISK ENTRY with documented 3A/3B escape hatch — KEEP-BUNDLED with crafter escalation gate during execution.
